title: pastinaca sativa subsp. umbrosa
intro: Pastinaca sativa subsp. umbrosa, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Pastinaca sativa subsp. umbrosa include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
